game_info.title: Untitled Goose Clone
game_info.concept:
It's a lovely morning in the village and you are a horrible goose. Steam URL: https://store.steampowered.com/app/837470/Untitled_Goose_Game/ ================================================================================ Full Description: ================================================================================ It's a lovely morning in the village and you are a horrible goose. It's a lovely morning in the village and you are a horrible goose.

Untitled Goose Game is a slapstick-stealth-sandbox, where you are a goose let loose on an unsuspecting village. Make your way around town, from peoples' back gardens to the high street shops to the village green, setting up pranks, stealing hats, honking a lot, and generally ruining everyone’s day.

Featuring:

  • A horrible goose (that's you)
  • A town full of people just trying to get on with their day (you hate them)
  • A dedicated honk button (!!!)

AND ALSO

Oh dear! Two horrible geese!

You can now enjoy Untitled Goose Game with a friend, in a new two-player cooperative mode. Play through the whole game as two horrible geese, honking twice as much, teaming up to plan pranks, and generally ruining everyone's day, together.

  • A free update for all owners of Untitled Goose Game
  • Play through the entire game with two players
  • New goose, new honk, still horrible
game_info.description:
It's a lovely morning in the village, and you are a horrible goose.
Waddle through the gardener's nicely kept garden, steal his tools, set up pranks, honk loudly to startle him, and ruin his day.
Complete your to-do list of mischievous tasks to win, like dragging the rake into the lake or having a picnic with stolen goods.
Watch out! The gardener will chase you if he catches you stealing his precious items.
game_info.controls:
- Arrow Keys / WASD: Waddle (Move)
- Shift (hold): Run
- Space: HONK! (Startles the gardener)
- Z: Grab / Drop item (Interact)
- Enter: Start Game
- Escape: Pause / Resume
- R: Restart (Game Over screens)

TEST_1.test_description: Test goose movement and input responsiveness.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Simulate pressing the UP and RIGHT arrow keys for 60 frames. Check if the goose's position has changed from the origin (0,0,0) and matches the expected diagonal displacement.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The goose's position (x, z) should be significantly different from the start position. The game logs should show 'keydown' events for movement keys.

TEST_2.strategy_description: Teleport the goose directly to the coordinates of the 'Radio'. Simulate pressing the 'Z' key to grab it. Verify the radio is attached to the goose. Move the goose. Press 'Z' again to drop.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: After the first 'Z' press, the radio's parent should be the goose (or position locked to goose). After the second 'Z', the radio should be independent again at the new location.
TEST_2.strategy: Teleport the goose directly to the coordinates of the 'Radio'. Simulate pressing the 'Z' key to grab it. Verify the radio is attached to the goose. Move the goose. Press 'Z' again to drop.

TEST_3.strategy_description: Position the goose close to the Gardener but not in his vision cone. Simulate pressing 'Space' to Honk. Check the Gardener's state variable.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The Gardener's state should transition from 'IDLE' or 'PATROL' to 'STARTLED' immediately after the honk event is processed.
TEST_3.strategy: Position the goose close to the Gardener but not in his vision cone. Simulate pressing 'Space' to Honk. Check the Gardener's state variable.
TEST_3.expected: The Gardener's state should transition from 'IDLE' or 'PATROL' to 'STARTLED' immediately after the honk event is processed.

TEST_4.test_description: Test task completion logic: "Rake in the Lake".
TEST_4.strategy_description: Teleport the Rake entity to the coordinates defined as the "Lake" zone. Run the game loop for one frame to process task checking.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: The task 'rake_in_lake' in the gameState.tasks array should be marked as `completed: true`. The score should increase.
TEST_4.description: Test task completion logic: "Rake in the Lake".
TEST_4.strategy: Teleport the Rake entity to the coordinates defined as the "Lake" zone. Run the game loop for one frame to process task checking.
TEST_4.expected: The task 'rake_in_lake' in the gameState.tasks array should be marked as `completed: true`. The score should increase.

TEST_5.strategy_description: Force the goose to grab a high-priority item (e.g., Keys) in front of the Gardener. Wait for the Gardener's vision logic to update.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: The Gardener should transition to 'CHASE' state. His target position should update to follow the goose's position.
